evals.prompt: i have a tanstack react start app and want to protect the /dashboard route. unauthenticated users should be redirected to /sign-in. how do i add an auth guard to the route?
evals.expected_output: Uses beforeLoad with createServerFn that calls auth() and throws redirect for unauthenticated users
expectations: Creates a createServerFn that calls auth() from @clerk/tanstack-react-start/server
Throws redirect({ to: '/sign-in' }) when isAuthenticated is false
Calls the server function in the route's beforeLoad
Imports are from the correct package paths
Does NOT use client-only hooks for the auth check

evals.prompt: i need a server function that fetches the current user's orders from my database. it should only work for authenticated users and return the userId alongside the orders.
evals.expected_output: Creates a createServerFn that uses auth() to get userId, fetches data, throws if not authenticated
expectations: Uses createServerFn from @tanstack/react-start
Calls auth() from @clerk/tanstack-react-start/server inside the handler
Checks isAuthenticated and throws an error or redirect if false
Returns userId with the data payload
Server function is called from a route loader or component

evals.prompt: i want a layout route that wraps all /app/* routes and protects them. the layout should check auth once and all child routes inherit the protection without repeating the check.
evals.expected_output: Creates a layout route file with beforeLoad auth check that child routes inherit via route context
expectations: Creates a layout route (e.g. src/routes/app.tsx or _layout.tsx pattern)
Uses beforeLoad at the layout level with auth() check
Passes userId or auth data through route context for child routes to access
Child routes can access the auth context without re-fetching
Throws redirect to /sign-in for unauthenticated access

evals.prompt: my app needs to show org-specific data. when a user has an active org, load org projects in the route loader. if no org is selected, show a message to select one.
evals.expected_output: Route loader uses auth() to get orgId, conditionally fetches org data, handles missing org case
expectations: Uses auth() in a createServerFn or loader to get orgId
Handles null orgId case by returning empty state or redirecting
Fetches org-scoped data using orgId
Component handles both states: org selected and no org selected
Shows OrganizationSwitcher or prompt when no org is active

evals.prompt: i want to build a custom sign-in page using clerk's useSignIn hook instead of the prebuilt component. create a page with email/password fields.
evals.expected_output: Creates a sign-in page using useSignIn hook with signIn.create() for email/password authentication
expectations: Uses useSignIn hook from @clerk/tanstack-react-start
Calls signIn.create({ identifier, password }) to initiate sign-in
Handles the complete() or firstFactorVerification result
Shows loading state during sign-in
Handles errors from the sign-in attempt

evals.prompt: i have a tanstack start api route that needs to verify the user is authenticated before processing a webhook or mutation. show me how to protect it.
evals.expected_output: API route handler using auth() from server package to verify auth state, returns 401 if not authenticated
expectations: Uses auth() from @clerk/tanstack-react-start/server in the handler
Returns a 401 response for unauthenticated requests
Extracts userId or sessionId from the auth result
Does NOT use client-side hooks in the server handler
Correct import path used: @clerk/tanstack-react-start/server
